threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the trains,' and you look genuinely surprised when they run late on weekends.
Heard a guy on the 59 last night arguing with a pigeon. Claimed it owed him a tenner for a lost bet. Pigeon wasn’t backing down.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a cat casually lounging on a window sill while a street musician played jazz below. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here can you fill up your water bottle at the cute little 'Brunnen' fountains scattered around, while dodging the tram tracks in the middle of the street.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You'll spot a true local if they roll their eyes at tourists waiting at the BART station, blissfully unaware it’s been delayed for the third time this week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard a saxophonist jamming on the 21 bus, the perfect soundtrack to the sunset over Market. Only in SF do commutes come with a front-row seat to talent.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think we all love paella. You won’t find it on many Gràcia menus; it’s a beach thing, not a neighborhood staple.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the lack of pedestrian crossings on Carrer Verdi, even when it’s bustling with locals.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is on time. Locals know it’s just a suggestion and plan their lives around those delays.
Just spotted a fellow on the 36 singing opera like he was back in the Royal Albert Hall. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really in Neukölln when I spotted a kid selling homemade cookies outside the U-Bahn station, and nobody batted an eye.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see spontaneous techno parties in abandoned factories. It’s a unique blend of creativity and chaos you won’t find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You’ll spot a local if they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center BART at rush hour, even before all the smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling at the Civic Center stop while a bus driver was handing out snacks. Only in San Francisco do transit delays turn into street performances.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is a photo op, but locals see them as echo chambers for street musicians. There’s a rhythm to it, different every day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewildered look at the metro map, still trying to wrap their head around the L3 and L5 connections.
